title: Greek Salad
subtitle: Classic horiatiki with tomatoes, cucumber, olives, and feta
excerpt: Greek salad is a simple combination of ripe tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, green pepper, kalamata olives, and a block of feta dressed with good olive oil and dried oregano. There is no lettuce and the dressing is just oil; no vinegar. It is at its best in summer when tomatoes are at their peak.
sourceNotes: Horiatiki, meaning village salad, is one of the most recognised dishes in Greek cuisine. The recipe is straightforward and varies little across the country: ripe vegetables, olives, feta, olive oil, and dried oregano. Vinegar is not traditional in the Greek version; some add a little lemon juice.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best assembled and dressed just before serving. You can cut the vegetables 30 minutes ahead and refrigerate, then assemble to serve.

content.text: Greek salad (horiatiki) is tomatoes, cucumber, green pepper, red onion, kalamata olives, and a thick slab of feta dressed with good olive oil and dried oregano. No lettuce, no dressing as such, no vinegar in the traditional version. The tomatoes are the dish; buy the best you can find.

items.prepNote: large, ripe, cut into rough wedges
left as a large slab, not crumbled

content.text: Arrange the tomato wedges in a wide bowl or on a large plate. Add the cucumber, red onion rings, green pepper, and olives.
Place the feta slab on top whole. Drizzle generously with olive oil. Scatter the dried oregano over everything, including the feta. Add a pinch of sea salt flakes.
Serve immediately with bread to mop the oil and tomato juices. The feta is typically broken at the table rather than crumbled in advance.

items.cause: Tomatoes and cucumber release juice once cut and salted
items.fix: Assemble and dress at the last moment. If cutting ahead, keep the tomatoes and cucumber separate and don't salt them until you're ready to serve.

items.symptom: The feta crumbles when I try to place it as a slab
items.cause: Feta from a block rather than in brine, or cheap crumbly variety
items.fix: Buy feta packed in brine; it stays more intact. Handle it gently and lift it straight from the packaging to the salad.

items.cause: Out-of-season tomatoes, or not enough olive oil
items.fix: The tomatoes are everything here. In winter, use the best cherry tomatoes you can find. Use a good olive oil and be generous with it.

content.text: Horiatiki is the salad of Greek summers. It appears at every taverna, on every family table through June to September, eaten alongside grilled fish or meat or simply with bread as a light meal. Outside Greece it has become one of the best-known Greek dishes. The version here is the traditional one: no lettuce, no dressing beyond oil, feta served as a slab not crumbled.
